extra.reasoning: The property G(opted_out implies not sent) is VIOLATED. Transition t16 (on send_attempt if opted_out and stage>=4: sent := true) sets sent true while opted_out is true, which directly contradicts the property — provided the state opted_out=true, stage=4 is reachable. It is reachable. The stage variable acts as a 4-step combination lock advanced by key_0 (0->1, t3), key_2 (1->2, t6), key_0 (2->3, t9), key_2 (3->4, t12). The optout transition t1 (guard true) sets opted_out:=true without touching stage, and the stage-advancing transitions guard only on stage (not on opted_out) and set sent:=false. So we can opt out first, then climb the ladder. Trace: (1) optout -> opted_out=true, sent=false, stage=0. (2) key_0 [t3] -> stage=1, sent=false. (3) key_2 [t6] -> stage=2, sent=false. (4) key_0 [t9] -> stage=3, sent=false. (5) key_2 [t12] -> stage=4, sent=false. (6) send_attempt: not opted_out fails (t2/t11), opted_out and stage<4 fails (t15, stage=4), opted_out and stage>=4 holds so t16 fires -> sent=true. Final state opted_out=true AND sent=true violates opted_out implies not sent. This is 6 events, within the 7-event bound.
